raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "\"Reframe the conceptual story\" is a direct instruction to rework the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — this dimension is the literal load-bearing deliverable; failure to reconstruct the conceptual core invalidates the delegation entirely.",
    "R1.4": "Any reframing of a scientific contribution story requires repositioning the delta against prior work; the artifact contains a live ambiguity here (BenchVar-as-benchmark vs. LLM-judge-validation-as-contribution), making novelty assessment central to getting the reframe right.",
    "R1.5": "Shifting the conceptual frame typically demands restructuring the narrative arc — where the gap is introduced, what the punchline is, and how pipeline results are subordinated to or elevated above judge-validation results — placing narrative reorganization at a central expected responsibility."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the reframe reposition the primary contribution claim itself — specifically, treating BenchVar (the benchmark artifact) rather than the LLM-judge validation as the central novel contribution — or should it preserve the existing contribution hierarchy and only reshape the conceptual framing and narrative arc around that same hierarchy?"
}

rationale.R1.1: "Reframe the conceptual story" is a direct instruction to rework the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — this dimension is the literal load-bearing deliverable; failure to reconstruct the conceptual core invalidates the delegation entirely.
rationale.R1.4: Any reframing of a scientific contribution story requires repositioning the delta against prior work; the artifact contains a live ambiguity here (BenchVar-as-benchmark vs. LLM-judge-validation-as-contribution), making novelty assessment central to getting the reframe right.
rationale.R1.5: Shifting the conceptual frame typically demands restructuring the narrative arc — where the gap is introduced, what the punchline is, and how pipeline results are subordinated to or elevated above judge-validation results — placing narrative reorganization at a central expected responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the reframe reposition the primary contribution claim itself — specifically, treating BenchVar (the benchmark artifact) rather than the LLM-judge validation as the central novel contribution — or should it preserve the existing contribution hierarchy and only reshape the conceptual framing and narrative arc around that same hierarchy?
